definitions: A shot in which the camera is placed below the subject and angled upward, making the subject appear larger, more powerful, or dominant.
A cinematic technique used to emphasize the height or importance of a character or object.
examples: The director used a Low-Angle Shot to make the villain seem more menacing.
A Low-Angle Shot can create a sense of awe.
The Low-Angle Shot highlighted the skyscraper's immense size.
